evals.prompt: Set up this repo for the kit.
evals.expected_output: Reads the repo and generates the repo-local envelope under .agents/envelope/ (linear-map.md, domain.md, commands.md, and templates/ stamped from blueprint's templates), discovering the Linear team/project/label/state mapping and asking only for facts no tool can supply, create-missing-only and without writing secrets. Does not create Linear issues, branches, or PRs.

evals.prompt: Refresh the Linear mapping for this repo — we moved to a new team and added a few labels.
evals.expected_output: Refreshes linear-map.md in the existing envelope by re-discovering the Linear team/project/labels/states and proposing a confirmed diff rather than clobbering the existing file, leaving the other bindings intact.

evals.prompt: set up this rebo for the factorio kit, dunno what comands or labels it uses yet
evals.expected_output: Still activates despite typos: inspects the repo to detect build/test/lint commands, discovers the Linear mapping via the list tools, asks only for the gaps it cannot infer, and stamps the envelope bindings create-missing-only.

evals.prompt: Create an issue for the login bug.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT activate as setup; routes to ghosts, since assembler maps the tracker but never opens Linear issues.

evals.prompt: Write the PRD for offline mode and give me a PR template.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT own spec or template content; routes to blueprint, which owns the canonical templates assembler later stamps into the repo envelope.

evals.prompt: While setting up this repo for the kit, also scaffold a repo-specific skill for our recurring nightly data-import workflow.
evals.expected_output: As part of repo setup, scaffolds a repo-specific skill under the repo's own .agents/skills/<repo>-<capability>/ (one level deep, concrete Use-when trigger, reads the envelope, cites the generic kit skill it specializes), only because a real recurring workflow justifies it; passes validate-skills. Generic standalone skill authoring is write-a-skill's job; assembler owns repo-specific scaffolding from the envelope during setup/refresh.

evals.prompt: Is this repo kit-ready? Check the envelope.
evals.expected_output: Runs the verify checklist: confirms all four bindings exist and agree, every inserter role maps to a real state/label, commands are real, the AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md agent-skills block points at .agents/envelope/, and reports kit-ready or the exact gaps. Does not overwrite anything.
